zotero.abstractNote: Generalist policies, trained on diverse datasets, have shown remarkable capabilities in robotics. However, adapting these policies to new, unseen tasks remains a challenge. Standard fine-tuning often requires large amounts of task-specific data, which is expensive to collect. In this work, we propose Active Fine-Tuning (AFT), a framework that enables generalist policies to efficiently adapt to new tasks by actively selecting the most informative data for fine-tuning. We introduce a novel acquisition function based on the expected information gain about the optimal policy parameters. We demonstrate that AFT significantly outperforms standard fine-tuning and other active learning baselines in various robotic manipulation tasks, requiring fewer environment interactions to achieve high performance.
